title: pectis discoidea
intro: Pectis discoidea, also known as Coyote Brush, is a shrubby plant species belonging to the daisy family that is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. Pectis discoidea has been reported to have various health benefits, such as treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating digestive issues. The plant can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
